description: Self-TARGET onTransition is the error arm of the .SameState trichotomy (§51.0.F.1). Inside <Loading rule=.Done>, <onTransition to=.Loading> means 'fire on LEAVING toward .Loading' — but there is no .Loading -> .Loading edge, so it is a hard E-ENGINE-INVALID-TRANSITION (NOT a self-write no-op, NOT the info lint). Contrast engine-self-write-pos (the no-op arm).
rationale: §51.0.F.1 a self-TARGET <onTransition to=.Loading> inside <Loading> is a hard E-ENGINE-INVALID-TRANSITION (there is no self-edge); the discriminator is whether a real entry occurs — a to= handler asserts a non-existent edge.
